signals.headline: Election Commission Announces Schedule for 2026 Kerala Assembly Elections | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Election Commission Announces Schedule for 2026 Kerala Assembly Elections | YES | MED confidence**

The Election Commission of India confirmed the schedule for the 2026 Kerala legislative assembly elections (along with Assam, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal) on Sunday, setting the polling date for April 9 in Kerala[4]. signals.headline: US-Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Agreed (April 8)[2]** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
US-Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Agreed (April 8)[2]** | YES | HIGH confidence
The United States, Israel, and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Wednesday, April 8, 2026, described as an "11th-hour deal" to prevent wider regional war.[2] This would move prediction markets on Middle East conflict escalation.

signals.headline: Key Unresolved Issues:** Iran's nuclear program remains the central sticking point, with Washington demanding strict lim
signals.raw: Key Unresolved Issues:** Iran's nuclear program remains the central sticking point, with Washington demanding strict limits while Iran seeks recognition of its rights.[1] The ceasefire deadline remains unannounced, and ship navigation in the Strait of Hormuz is still suspended despite the ceasefire agreement.[2]
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?

signals.headline: Trump Signals Breakthrough Possible in US-Iran Peace Negotiations (April 16)[1]** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Trump Signals Breakthrough Possible in US-Iran Peace Negotiations (April 16)[1]** | YES | HIGH confidence
Donald Trump declared that a US-Iran peace deal is "very possible," with Washington confirming active negotiations are entering a critical phase.[1] A second round of in-person talks may occur in Islamabad.[1] This affects markets on diplomatic resolution likelihood.

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 CPI confirms inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 CPI confirms inflation at 2.16% YoY, up from 2.00% in February | YES | HIGH confidence** [2][6]

South Korea's official Consumer Price Index for March 2026, released April 2, 2026, shows headline inflation rising to 2.16% year-over-year from 2.00% in February, with a 0.34% month-over-month increase.[2][6] Core inflation held at 2.50% YoY in February, above the 2.20% Q1 forec
